pi_items.source: ImageJ/Fiji built-in sample confirmed at https://imagej.net/ij/images/blobs.gif
File command analysis: 'GIF image data, version 89a, 256 x 254'
Actual particle count with Otsu threshold, inversion, and size >= 10 pixels filter: 62 particles
Actual particle count with Otsu threshold, inversion, and size >= 10 pixels filter: 62 particles
Average particle area with Otsu threshold, inversion, and size >= 10 pixels filter: 372.10 pixels²
Average particle area with Otsu threshold, inversion, and size >= 10 pixels filter: 372.10 pixels²
ImageJ documentation confirms 'Default' threshold uses Otsu's method (https://imagej.net/ij/docs/menus/analyze.html)
Standard ImageJ Analyze Particles size parameter. Analysis with size >= 10 pixels² yields 62 particles with mean area 372.1
File confirmed at official ImageJ repository; file properties show GIF89a format, 256x254 pixels, 25K size, last modified 2017-01-14

privileged_info_summary: The count_particles task uses the blobs.gif sample image from ImageJ, confirmed to be 256×254 pixels. Using the default (Otsu) threshold method and inverting the image to create white particles on black background, the image contains 62 particles when filtered by minimum size of 10 pixels². The average particle area is 372.1 pixels², with a range from 16.0 to 923.0 pixels². All expected value ranges in metadata (particle count 50-80, average area 100-500) are verified as accurate.
